programmes.show_description: This time it's Race Across the World, Married at First Sight Australia, The Apprentice, Last One Laughing, Secrets of the Bees, Just One Thing and Celebrity Ex on the Beach.
Gamer Lauren is looking for her perfect player number two to help her reboot her love life. And Ryan is searching for a silver fox with a dad bod.
...Experiment: Chelsea and George seek a threesome, but will it help Chelsea move past George's sexually adventurous past and infidelity? Jess and Ben host a naked dinner party.
Josh Pugh, Lorraine Kelly and Steve Bracknall join Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker for a tongue-in-cheek look at the week's news.
In South Wales, there's a power tussle in the kitchen, a shared appreciation of bargains, and a few home truths, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free
programmes.show_description: Kirstie Allsopp and the team come to the rescue of Dave and Kate, whose house has been flooded.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George meets builder Anthony and physiotherapist Emily, who are determined to turn their ramshackle 17th-century cottage in Warwickshire into their family home.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Affairs of the Heart: When a classy customer falls for Carla, no one can work out why she's not interested. But it seems she's convinced that he must have some terrible flaw.
Old Flames: Sam's old buddy Dave insists that he can break up Sam's romance with Diane in just 24 hours, especially when Diane discovers Sam still has his 'little black book'.
Manager Coach: When Coach gets the chance to manage a youth league baseball team, his competitive nature soon turns him from a loveable guy into an angry tyrant.
Your Place or Mine?: When Ray's parents fall out, his mum moves in with him and Debra. But Debra isn't happy and tries to convince Ray's dad to apologise.
In-Laws: Debra's well-to-do parents come to visit. Ray begs his own parents to be nice to them, but when they all go out for a meal, it proves a tall order...
Win, Lose or Draw: Ray decides to ignore his dad's poker advice in order to prove he doesn't need any help. But when he loses yet again, Debra wants him to ask for his money back.
It Takes Two to Tangle: Frasier and Niles desperately search for a wealthy patron to lend support to the failing prep school they both attended.
Forgotten But Not Gone: Frasier resigns from his beloved wine club to avoid a conflict of interest when he begins a new radio show about wine.
Daphne Returns: Niles's romantic chances could be scuppered when Daphne's return sparks a squabble that could end it all.
The Wizard and Roz: Frasier seeks advice from his mentor, who seems more interested in Roz. Daphne tries to convince Niles that she's psychic.
Barefoot Bob's: Gordon visits Barefoot Bob's beachside restaurant in Massachusetts, where husband and wife Marc and Lisa struggle to attract customers during the winter months.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Leyland, Simon O'Brien transforms three battered car seats into a three-piece suite. And Henry Cole repurposes an overhead light into a fashionable light box.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: The Bakers call in some local storm-proofing experts to safeguard the farmhouse and Matt takes his dad to see the progress that's been made on his 1946 Dodge.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Anne-Marie and Adrian consider Los Cristianos in southern Tenerife their second home... so why not buy one there? Can Jean Johansson find the perfect place for £160k?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Spain, demolitions get underway at Tom and Terry's B&B. And eight years of building works finish in France, but can Paul and Fiona successfully transition into hosting?
The new series continues. Terry and Ashley finish their suite and rehang a 200-crystal chandelier. Belinda and Lee tackle tower mould. Can Vivienne and Simon complete their mural?
...the Sun: With Jon back to health, things finally start to look up. Work starts in the garden and Jasmine's carpenter brother Andrew helps out making bookshelves for Joy's bedroom. (Ep8)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programmes.show_name: New: Help! I Bought It at...
programmes.show_description: ...Auction with Sarah Beeny: In the South Downs National Park, Michelle and Derek take on a cosy cottage in need of some TLC. Archie and Lauren try to add value to their fixer-upper. (Ep3)
Claymore: Spy thriller with Simu Liu. When top-secret intelligence agency The Orphanage becomes convinced that it has a mole, analyst Alexander Hale realises he's the prime suspect. (Ep1/8)
Glass House: Spy thriller. Alexander and The Orphanage are forced to work together to protect their discovery, setting a deadly trap with Alexander and Michelle as bait. (Ep2/8)
Carol's rushed to Queen's after a lighter set fire to her oxygen tank and burnt her face. Trevor, who's blind, has fallen down the stairs at home. And Grace has injured her knee.
Headteacher Andy has had a cardiac arrest. Rob's trapped under a pallet of granite in Ipswich. Thirteen-week pregnant Katie's having seizures and the air medics are on their way.
Jack's Waterfront: In Michigan, Gordon Ramsay visits a lakeside restaurant in a summer hotspot, where the food fails to match the fantastic views. Can Gordon turn the tide?
The epic adventure reality contest continues. As alliances chop and change, a rookie Predator goes in ruthless pursuit of Prey. Will a last-minute plot change the course of the game? (Ep7)
The epic adventure reality contest continues. The gloves are off as the last five players embark on the most important hunt yet: the battle for a place in the final. Who will survive? (Ep8)
Kirstie Allsopp puts celebs to the test with crafting challenges. Wendi Peters and Ricky Groves take on lint-roller printing. Sharron Davies and Tanni Grey-Thompson have a banner craft-off.
Kirstie Allsopp helps sisters Gemma and Lucy find a home in costly Berkshire while Phil Spencer helps newly-weds Hannah and Hassan find a four-bed house in Buckinghamshire.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: Can Duncan and Josie transform their 18th-century barn into a liveable home before baby Ruby arrives? And can Jenny restore a Victorian gothic pile to its former glory?
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
They Called Me Mayday: When a TV chat show host visits the bar, Diane tries to interest him in her poetry, but it's Sam's life story that really catches his attention.
How Do I Love Thee, Let Me Call You Back: When Sam says 'I love you' to Diane without considering the consequences, she begins to analyse their relationship.
Just Three Friends: When Diane's old friend Heather comes to town, Sam thinks that she's fallen for him. Diane knows better but becomes paranoid when Carla taunts her about it.
Turkey or Fish: Debra takes it upon herself to prepare the Thanksgiving dinner, but she doesn't want her turkey to be compared with Marie's, so she cooks fish instead.
Captain Nemo: Ray's appointed captain of the basketball team after Robert is ousted, but their parents are not pleased about the situation.
The Ball: Ray's upset to discover that the autographed Mickey Mantle baseball his dad gave him years ago is a fake. So he decides to tell his daughter Ally the truth about Santa.
Semi-Decent Proposal: Frasier agrees to escort his former high school prom queen to her surprise birthday party - because he wants to spend time with her friend.
A Passing Fancy: Frasier tutors Lorna's son Kirby in exchange for a date with Claire, while Roz gets a day off as a reward for taking Kirby to the prom.
A Day in May: Frasier comforts a crestfallen husband whose house has been put up for sale after his wife abandoned him and took the children with her.
Cranes Go Caribbean: Frasier's horrified when he learns that Martin, Niles and Daphne are going to accompany him on his trip to Belize with Claire.
Gordon revisits Café Hon in Baltimore, Chiarella's in Philadelphia, and Leone's in New Jersey, to find out whether the owners have taken his advice on board.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Southport, Henry Cole finds some ammunition crates that he wants to turn into furniture, while Simon O'Brien plans to convert a light from Southport Pier into a table.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum & Dad
programmes.show_description: Matt Baker takes his mum Janice and dad Mike on memorable excursions around England's beautiful North East, starting with a behind-the-scenes visit to Bamburgh Castle Estate.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Tom and his son Alan look for a holiday home on beautiful Gran Canaria, that their whole family can enjoy. Can Ben Hillman find the perfect place for Tom's £120k budget?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Tom and Terry's Spanish B&B renovations reach a milestone. In Portugal, changes are afoot at Leah and Joao's cocktail bar. And in Italy, Summer and Ashley start their reno.
The new series continues. Tim and Krys build a wine cave, but is Tim out of his depth? Alicia and Carlton prep for their first wedding. Tim and Sasha create a colourful courtyard.
...the Sun: With spirits raised, it's full steam ahead and time to create the driveway. But installing the outside staircase doesn't go to plan! (Ep9)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Amazing Trains: Paul Merton takes charge of some iconic locomotives. On the Isle of Wight, Paul drives a heritage steam train and finds out how the island helped with D-Day. (Ep1)
Taskmaster's back! Amy Gledhill, Armando Iannucci, Joanna Page, Joel Dommett and Kumail Nanjiani leave all dignity at the door as they hunt for worms and feed Greg his five-a-day.
False Flag: Spy thriller with Simu Liu. With his fake life up and running, Alexander must decide if he's willing to sacrifice a real mission in order to hunt down the hackers. (Ep3/8)
Obsidian: Spy thriller. In a mission where nothing is as it seems, will Alexander risk his own life to save another? Meanwhile, Michelle gets a visit from the past. (Ep4/8)
